nodes.label: Tensile library type compatibility matrix on gfx803 Linux

attrs.safe_types: 4xii8I, BB, BS, CC, DD (normal speed, but noise on 6.4)
attrs.fast_types: HH, HS, I8I (6x speedup 1.7s/it -> 6it/s, reduced VRAM to 3GB, but noise)
attrs.crash_types: SS (causes segfault/MAF on any .hsaco, .co, or .dat file)

nodes.label: Tensile SS-type libraries cause segfault on gfx803 Linux
HH/HS/I8I Tensile types give 6x speedup but produce noise on gfx803
WorkGroupMappingType:A + WorkGroupMapping:0 yields ~1s/it improvement on Linux gfx803
AssertAIGreaterThanEqual in Tensile yaml can be safely commented out without affecting output
Custom rocblas on ROCm 6.4 gives ~1s/it speedup (6.5->5.5 SDXL) but still produces noise
KoboldCPP on ROCm 5.7 requires cudaStreamWaitEvent 3-arg fix and __hostdevice__ static fix
ROCm token processing 10x slower than Vulkan, GPU only draws 110W vs 170W on Vulkan
Desktop UI freezes periodically during ROCm token processing on gfx803
CLIP text length directly impacts it/s: 252 chars=1.5s/it, 24K chars=3.3s/it (512x512)
Iteration speed is unstable: first step ~6s/it then second step instant, avg 5.35s/it SDXL
Including r9nano Tensile folder alongside Polaris folder may be required for full coverage
Polaris folder may need to be lowercase 'polaris' to match other Tensile convention
Hypothesis: ROCm 6.4 noise on gfx803 is systematic (not fixable with flags or rocblas swap)
Hypothesis: SS-type Tensile kernels use ISA instructions incompatible with gfx803
repeated_inference_drift fail_nondeterministic on docker/itir:latest
Basic math (GroupNorm, SiLU, attention) works without NaN/Inf but ComfyUI still outputs noise
